task_name: Add Custom Theme System to T3 Code Editor

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete implementation plan I've put together after thoroughly exploring the codebase:

---

## Plan: Custom Theme System

**6 files total — 2 created, 4 modified. Zero new dependencies, zero build config changes.**

### What I found
- `packages/shared` uses direct-source TypeScript exports with no React/browser APIs — perfect for pure theme data
- `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` currently handles `"light" | "dark" | "system"` with a `useSyncExternalStore` pattern; `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` depends on its `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` output
- CSS already uses custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, etc.) which can be overridden by inline styles on `:root`
- The Settings page has an existing Appearance section (radio buttons) that will be replaced by the new `ThemeSelector`

### Files

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **Create** — `Theme`/`ThemeColors` interfaces, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme(id)`, `listThemes()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **Modify** — add `"./themes"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Replace** — new hook, same `useSyncExternalStore` pattern, returns `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes, resolvedTheme }` |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **Modify** — add font/size CSS variables + 4 new surface vars as defaults |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **Create** — radio-group with 3 color swatches per theme |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **Modify** — swap Appearance section for `<ThemeSelector />` |

### Key design decisions
- **New localStorage key** `t3code:theme-id` (avoids colliding with old `t3code:theme`), with migration: old `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"`/`"system"` → `"t3-dark"`
- **`resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"`** preserved on the hook (derived from `theme.dark: boolean`) so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` needs zero changes
- **CSS injection** via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty(...)` — inline styles beat stylesheet specificity, so all existing components pick up changes automatically
- **Module-load apply** (same as existing hook) prevents flash before React renders
- **`t3-midnight`** — deep navy background with cyan-blue accent as the third distinctive built-in theme
